title: The Boat of the Obedient
subtitle: Christ teaches from Simon's boat, and the nets break with the catch

stillness: Let the boat be drawn back a little from the shore.
Not far in body, but far in heart.
Here, where the Lord sits and teaches, be still.
gospel: One day, while the crowds pressed upon Him to hear the word of God,
He was standing beside the lake of Gennesaret,
which is also called the sea of Galilee, or of Tiberias.
He saw two boats standing beside the lake;
one was Simon's, and his brother Andrew's;
the other was James's and John's.
The fishermen had gone down to land and were washing their nets,
for that whole night they had caught nothing.
Going up into one boat, which was Simon's,
He asked him to draw the boat back a little from the land,
and sitting down, He taught the crowds from the boat.
When He had finished speaking He said to Simon:
'Put out into the deep, and let down your nets for a catch.'
Simon answered: 'Master, through the whole night,
but at Your word I will let down the net.'
And when they had done this, they enclosed a great multitude of fish.
behold: See here the wondrous humility and meekness of Christ.
He could have commanded, for He was the Teacher;
Behold Him sitting in the little boat, as one using the authority of a teacher,
teaching the crowds who stand upon the land.
He willed the boat to be drawn back from the shore,
so that He might be heard, yet not be crushed.
Mark this: to all it is said, let down your nets in the humility of Simon;
but to Peter alone is it said, put out into the deep.
consider: The crowd pressed upon Him and pushed against Him,
out of too great a devotion and longing to hear the word of God.
Have you ever rushed upon Him so?
By this lake, which is called a sea, the world is understood:
which swells like the sea through pride,
boils through avarice,
foams through lust.
Through this world Christ saw, that is, approved, two boats.
The one not named is the common way of the commandments,
for all without distinction are bound to it.
The other, the named one, is Simon's,
and Simon is interpreted: the obedient one.
To this boat Christ descends, and sits, and teaches.
For Christ descends by grace to the heart of the religious who devoutly keeps His counsels,
He sits there by contemplation,
and He teaches by the inflowing of the gifts of the Holy Spirit.
He wills the boat to be drawn back from the land a little —
in heart, though not in body —
for our heart cannot otherwise be held
but that it touches the earth on some side.
Yet today many, by entering religion, are not separated nor drawn back from earthly things,
but rather draw nearer to them than before.
The fishermen wash their nets:
that is, the stains of sin contracted in preaching,
they wash away with the water of contrition.
For the words of preaching are often stained
by temporal gain, by empty glory, by deceiving flattery.
Then Simon labored the whole night and caught nothing —
yet at the word of Christ, trusting in the power of His word, he let down the net.
And here is given to the religious the form of obedience:
that they obey readily at a simple word,
not waiting for a command or a threat.
For to those subject to Him all the birds of heaven and the fish of the sea are subject;
the creatures are given into their power.
prayer: make me rush upon the nets of preaching and of desire and of good work
through eagerness to hear the word of God;
to wash them clean of all avarice, flattery, and empty glory;
to draw back the boat of religion from earthly things,
and to teach others by example,
and to lead into the deep of contemplation and of preaching;
to let down the nets at the word of Your inspiration,
and in the waters of tribulations
to enclose a copious multitude of inward consolations.
Call and draw me, a wretched man, to Your holy discipleship,
that, leaving all things and following You,
I may deserve to attain the fellowship of Your poor.

latinJewel.latin: Ad transeundum istum mundum vidit Christus, id est approbavit duas naves.
latinJewel.english: To cross over this world, Christ saw — that is, approved — two boats.
